message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Given a downward age shift in COVID-19-involved deaths observed during the COVID-19 pandemic, we sought to estimate years of life lost (YLL) associated with leading causes of US death during the first 20 months of the pandemic.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Despite 4796 fewer COVID-19 deaths in Jan-Oct 2021 than in Mar-Dec 2020, the number of YLL due to COVID-19 increased by 1,159,761, from 4,474,186 to 5,633,947 (a 25.9% increase). YLL per COVID-19 death increased from 12.8 in 2020 to 16.3 in 2021, a 27.7% increase. YLL per death did not change by more than 2.3% for any other cause.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Interpretation</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Increased YLL per COVID-19 death in 2021 result from younger-age COVID-19 mortality, contributing to a marked increase in YLL from this preventable cause of death at a later stage of the pandemic despite advancements in vaccines in treatments.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Years of Life Lost in the United States During the COVID-19 Pandemic, March 2020 to October 2021

reference.unstructured: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. Excess Deaths Associated with COVID-19. National Center for Health Statistics. Published July 6, 2022. Accessed July 8, 2022. https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nvss/vsrr/covid19/excess_deaths.htm

National Center for Health Statistics. National Vital Statistics System - Life Expectancy. US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. Published April 25, 2022. Accessed July 7, 2022. https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nvss/life-expectancy.htm
